Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (assault against persons who provide health services and first responders)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (assault against persons who provide health services and first responders)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to require a court to consider the fact that the victim of an assault is a person who provides health services or a first responder to be an aggravating circumstance for the purposes of sentencing.
And whereas persons who provide health services and first responders, who care for and protect others, deserve to feel protected and valued by the justice system;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after section 269.01:

Section.MarginalNote: Aggravating circumstance â assault against persons who provide health services and first responders
Section.Text: When a court imposes a sentence for an offence referred to in paragraph 264.1(1)(a) or any of sections 266 to 269, it shall consider as an aggravating circumstance the fact that the victim of the offence was, at the time of the commission of the offence, a person who provides health services, including personal care services, or a first responder engaged in the performance of their duty.
